reply_content: Kreese was suffering even before he went to Vietnam. His mother killed herself and he was an outcast as a result. Being many decades before the show began, the blooming stigma surrounding mental health prevented him from properly dealing with his issues, and his treatment of Johnny and getting his ass whooped by Miyagi in retaliation for trying to murder Johnny, all of these things, which he didn't address, pushed him off the slippery slope. Sadly though, because of his current actions, his trauma and past suffering is no longer an excuse for everything he has done. While he might have been in the wrong for rearing Tory and Johnny, he still had good intentions because he wanted to be the parental figure in their lives they never had, and while Johnny may have realized what Kreese has done to him, Tory hasn't, simply because she was trying to get over her mother's unexpected death. Kreese knew how to deal with her, but so did Johnny, because of how their mothers died. While Johnny wanted her to keep fighting, the Larussos didn't want their daughter to get hurt, which is why they stopped her from fairly winning, but Tory believed it was nepotism, and decided to side with Kreese. Hopefully, she will be able to make peace with everyone at Miyagi Do, and they'll forgive her. Whether or not Kreese or Silver will get their redemption arc is debatable, because of their actions, as while Kreese is taking responsibility, Silver is essentially a demon because of having tortured Daniel and his friends. Kim though, I really do hope she gets a redemption arc, better than her American friends, since Kwon's death likely made her realize that making her life all about karate, spending it under her granddad's thumb, was hardly even worth it, and that she can no longer hide her trauma from Tory, or deal with her granddad's wrath. Perhaps by the end of the series, Tory will hopefully forgive Kim for everything, but not either Kreese or Silver.
